title: Thermal Equilibrium Chamber
description: Two shader balls represent thermodynamic bodies inside a sealed chamber. The user manipulates heat sources, insulation barriers, and material conductivity to drive the system toward or away from thermal equilibrium, observing real-time heat transfer governed by conservation of energy. Undo/redo lets the user rewind any configuration step.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements a thermal equilibrium simulation. Load ShaderBall.glb on the left and ShaderBall2.glb on the right, centered in a transparent wireframe box representing a sealed chamber. Camera: perspective at (0,2,6) looking at origin with OrbitControls. Lighting: one ambient light (0.3 intensity) plus two point lights whose color and intensity reflect each ball's current temperature.

Each ball has a temperature value (0-1000 K) displayed on a floating HTML label above it. Implement conservation of energy: total energy across both balls plus the environment is constant. Heat transfers between balls at a rate proportional to their temperature difference multiplied by a conductivity slider value.

Interaction steps the user can perform: (1) Click a ball to select it. (2) Use a 'Heat Inject' slider to add energy to the selected ball (raises its temperature, lowers environment reserve). (3) Use a 'Cool Extract' slider to remove energy. (4) Adjust a 'Conductivity' slider (0-1) controlling transfer rate between balls. (5) Toggle an insulation barrier (a visible translucent plane between the balls) that blocks heat transfer when active. (6) Click a 'Step Simulation' button to advance one discrete time step of heat exchange. (7) Click 'Auto Run' to continuously simulate. (8) Use Undo button to revert the last state change. (9) Use Redo button to re-apply. (10) A 'Reset' button restores initial conditions.

Visually map temperature to ball emissive color: blue (cold) through red (hot) using a lerp on the material's emissive property. The wireframe chamber subtly pulses based on total system energy. Display a real-time bar chart (HTML/CSS overlay) showing energy distribution: Ball A, Ball B, Environment. Ensure energy bars always sum to the same constant total, enforcing conservation. Store a history stack of state snapshots (temperatures, slider values, barrier state) for undo/redo with a max depth of 50.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  leftBallTemp: number,         // temperature of left ball in Kelvin (0-1000)
  rightBallTemp: number,         // temperature of right ball in Kelvin (0-1000)
  environmentEnergy: number,         // reserve energy pool in the system (Joules)
  conductivity: number,         // heat transfer coefficient between balls (0-1)
  insulationActive: boolean,         // whether insulation barrier blocks heat transfer
  selectedBall: string,         // which ball is selected ('left'|'right'|null)
  heatInjectionRate: number,         // energy added per frame to selected ball (0-100)
  coolExtractionRate: number,         // energy removed per frame from selected ball (0-100)
  totalSystemEnergy: number,         // sum of all energy in system for conservation check
  equilibriumReached: boolean,         // true when temperature difference < 5K for 3+ seconds
}
